provenance: OpenCV Apache-2.0 (>=4.5); scikit-image BSD-3-Clause. Knowledge.
notes: VisionServeX uses plain OpenCV Canny/Sobel + scipy morphology (no contrib StructuredEdgeDetection NC model). Commercial-safe as implemented.

provenance: Dependency declared in pyproject.toml (opencv-python-headless>=4.8); OpenCV core relicensed BSD-3 -> Apache-2.0 at v4.5.0. Knowledge.
notes: Pure algorithm, no checkpoint. cv2.grabCut uses OpenCV's OWN internal min-cut/max-flow (gcgraph.hpp), NOT PyMaxflow, so no GPL is pulled in. GrabCut had a historical Microsoft patent but OpenCV ships it under Apache-2.0 and it is widely used commercially. Commercial-safe.

provenance: OpenCV main repo (modules/imgproc), Apache-2.0 since 4.5.0. Knowledge.
notes: Live-Wire / Intelligent Scissors. Shipped in OpenCV core (not contrib/nonfree) under Apache-2.0. The original Mortensen-Barrett livewire patent (US 5,995,115, filed 1997) is expired, and OpenCV distributes the implementation freely. No weights, no GPL. Commercial-safe.

code_license: BSD-3-Clause (scikit-learn RandomForest; optional skimage features BSD-3)
provenance: scikit-learn BSD-3-Clause; scikit-image BSD-3-Clause. Knowledge.
notes: Interactive per-pixel RandomForest classifier trained on user scribbles at runtime (model fit on the fly, no pretrained checkpoint shipped). All deps permissive BSD-3. Commercial-safe.

provenance: OpenCV Apache-2.0 (>=4.5); scikit-image BSD-3-Clause. Knowledge.
notes: Marker-controlled watershed. Either backend (OpenCV or skimage) is permissive. No weights, no GPL. Commercial-safe.

code_license: BSD-3-Clause (skimage.segmentation.random_walker; scipy BSD-3, optional pyamg MIT)
provenance: scikit-image BSD-3-Clause; scipy BSD-3; pyamg MIT. Knowledge.
notes: Random-walker segmentation. All deps permissive (BSD/MIT). Optional pyamg multigrid solver is MIT. No weights, no GPL. Commercial-safe.

code_license: BSD-3-Clause (skimage SLIC) + GPL-3.0 (PyMaxflow) — BK maxflow algorithm is research/non-commercial
provenance: WebSearch verified: PyMaxflow is GPL (http://pmneila.github.io/PyMaxflow/); underlying Boykov-Kolmogorov maxflow is non-commercial, commercial license via UCLB (https://xip.uclb.com/product/maxflow_computervision).
notes: VisionServeX implementation uses OpenCV grabCut (Apache-2.0 internal min-cut) + scikit-image SLIC (BSD-3). It does NOT use PyMaxflow/gco (GPL/non-commercial). Commercial-safe as implemented (verified in smart_annotation/classic/methods.py).

provenance: scikit-image BSD-3-Clause; scikit-learn BSD-3-Clause. Knowledge.

Apply for an API token at cloud.deepdataspace.com/apply-token (free starter quota, then paid via dashboard/WeChat Pay). Terms name = DeepDataSpace / IDEA Research API ToS. CODE-vs-WEIGHTS landmine: the Apache-2.0 license covers ONLY the DINO-X-API client repo, not the model; the DINO-X checkpoint is closed/proprietary and served remotely. Commercial use is gated behind paid API quota + provider ToS -> 'conditional'. No downloadable weights -> redistribution 'no'; BYOT (bring-your-own API token).

weights_license: Unspecified for checkpoints; mixed backbones (HRNet permissive; SegFormer/MiT non-commercial)
provenance: XavierCHEN34/ClickSEG — MIT code base (framework follows RITM) bundling CDNet (ICCV 2021) and FocalClick (CVPR 2022). Checkpoints publicly downloadable (Google Drive), not gated. Trained on COCO+LVIS (+ SBD configs).
notes: Toolbox/codebase rather than a single model. Code MIT (confirmed). Same dual weight concern as FocalClick: COCO+LVIS/SBD training-data terms plus the SegFormer/MiT backbone NVIDIA non-commercial license on the transformer variants; CDNet/HRNet variants are backbone-clean but still inherit dataset provenance. Per-checkpoint review required before commercial use/redistribution.

weights_license: Unspecified for checkpoints; best variants use SegFormer/MiT backbone under NVIDIA non-commercial source-code license
provenance: FocalClick (Xi Chen et al., CVPR 2022), distributed within XavierCHEN34/ClickSEG (MIT, framework derived from RITM). Checkpoints publicly downloadable (Google Drive), not gated. Trained on COCO+LVIS / SBD. Strongest models use SegFormer MiT-B0/B3 encoders.
notes: Code MIT. Two weight landmines: (1) training data COCO+LVIS/SBD provenance (Flickr-terms images); (2) the high-accuracy SegFormer/MiT backbone is ImageNet-pretrained under NVIDIA's non-commercial research license, so those checkpoints likely carry an NC restriction — HRNet-18 variants avoid this. Downloadable, not user-supplied-only. Treat SegFormer-backbone checkpoints as non-commercial until cleared; HRNet variants need only dataset review.

weights_license: Unspecified (no explicit checkpoint license); checkpoints trained on SBD and COCO+LVIS
provenance: SamsungLabs/ritm_interactive_segmentation. Code MIT (confirmed). Checkpoints publicly downloadable (Google Drive / weights folder), NOT HF-gated, no API key. Backbones HRNet/ResNet (ImageNet-pretrained). Trained on SBD and COCO+LVIS.
notes: Code is clean MIT. Commercial risk is on the WEIGHTS, not code: checkpoints trained on COCO+LVIS (images under Flickr terms; annotations CC BY 4.0) and SBD (derived from PASCAL VOC, Flickr-sourced) — no explicit commercial grant on the resulting weights. Checkpoints are downloadable (not user-supplied-only), but bundling/mirroring them commercially needs review. Recommend BYOT or legal sign-off before commercial redistribution.

weights_license: Effectively non-commercial: checkpoints initialized from Facebook MAE ViT weights (CC BY-NC 4.0)
provenance: uncbiag/SimpleClick (ICCV 2023). Code MIT (confirmed). README requires downloading Facebook MAE-pretrained ViT-B/L/H weights as backbone init; trained checkpoints downloadable from Google Drive (not gated). Trained on COCO+LVIS.
notes: Decisive landmine: MAE pretrained ViT backbones (facebookresearch/mae) are CC BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) — confirmed. SimpleClick checkpoints are derivatives of those NC weights, so the published weights are non-commercial regardless of the MIT code, and also carry COCO+LVIS dataset terms. Per rule 5, non-commercial => external_baseline_only. Do NOT bundle/redistribute weights commercially; use only as an external/research baseline.

weights_license: unknown (blog claims Apache-2.0; HF repo returns HTTP 401 / auth-restricted — unverifiable)
provenance: HF Roboflow/rf-detr-seg-2xlarge model page AND api/models endpoint consistently return HTTP 401 Unauthorized (cannot read license/gated metadata). Roboflow blog 'Nano..2XLarge all Apache 2.0' conflicts with the 401; detection-2XL is PML-1.0 (DINOv3, Roboflow account).
notes: Could NOT verify: the 2xlarge seg repo returns a consistent 401 (appears private/auth-restricted or not yet publicly released at audit time), which contradicts the blog's blanket Apache-2.0 claim and parallels the PML-1.0 detection-2XL (DINOv3, account-gated). Per commercial-safe policy this is unresolved -> legal review before any bundling. If confirmed publicly Apache + ungated, upgrade to core_allowed=yes/commercial=yes; if confirmed gated, treat as gated_byot_only with redistribution_allowed=no.

provenance: GitHub chongzhou96/EdgeSAM LICENSE fetched = S-Lab License 1.0; clause: 'Redistribution and use for non-commercial purpose ... permitted ... for commercial purpose ... please contact the contributor(s)'. Web-verified 2026-06.
notes: PRIMARY LANDMINE. EdgeSAM (S-Lab, NTU; chongzhou96) is licensed under NTU S-Lab License 1.0, which is NON-COMMERCIAL / research-only: it permits redistribution and use ONLY for non-commercial purposes and requires contacting the authors for any commercial use. This applies to BOTH the code and the edge_sam / edge_sam_3x checkpoints. It is NOT Apache-2.0. For a commercial-safe package: treat as external baseline only; do NOT bundle/mirror the weights for commercial distribution. ACTION: VisionServeX src/visionservex/model_zoo/manifest.py currently states 'EdgeSAM is Apache-2.0.' — this is INCORRECT and must be corrected to S-Lab License 1.0 (non-commercial).

weights_license: Apache-2.0 (declared) but trained on NON-COMMERCIAL data (HQSeg-44K)
provenance: GitHub SysCV/sam-hq LICENSE = Apache-2.0; HQSeg-44K composition + DIS5K Terms-of-Use (non-commercial) and ThinObject-5K (CC-BY-NC-4.0) web-verified 2026-06.
notes: LANDMINE (training data). Code Apache-2.0 and base SAM Apache-2.0. The HQ-output-token weights are fine-tuned on HQSeg-44K, which aggregates explicitly NON-COMMERCIAL datasets: DIS5K (research/educational-only ToU), ThinObject-5K (CC-BY-NC-4.0), plus academic saliency sets (ECSSD, MSRA-10K, DUT-OMRON). Weights are mirrorable (Apache-declared, open, not gated) but COMMERCIAL USE of the trained HQ weights is encumbered by the non-commercial training data => legal review before commercial deployment.

Backbone (MobileSAM) is commercial-safe, but the HQ token is trained on HQSeg-44K (DIS5K non-commercial + ThinObject-5K CC-BY-NC-4.0), so the trained HQ weights carry the same non-commercial encumbrance => legal review for commercial use. provenance: Verified web: huggingface.co/facebook/sam3 (HF gate) + facebook/sam3 LICENSE file (SAM License text)
notes: Access mechanism = Hugging Face gated access request: user must agree to share contact info (name, DOB, country, affiliation, job title) and be approved by Meta, then authenticate to download. Terms name = 'SAM License' (Meta custom, NOT Apache-2.0; SAM/SAM2 were Apache but SAM 3 changed to this custom license). Same license covers code + weights. Commercial use is permitted BUT subject to acceptable-use restrictions (no ITAR/military/warfare, nuclear, espionage, guns/illegal weapons, sanctioned uses) and redistribution must carry the agreement -> classed 'conditional' not 'yes'. Because weights are HF-gated, the package cannot mirror/bundle them: each user must accept the gate themselves -> redistribution 'no', BYOT (bring-your-own-gated-download).

weights_license: Mixed: Apache-2.0 (default 'total' task) / NON-COMMERCIAL (high-res, tissue_types, heartchambers_highres, face, appendicular_bones)
provenance: github.com/wasserth/TotalSegmentator (code Apache-2.0); README/LICENSE: results of appendicular_bones, tissue_types, heartchambers_highres and face 'may not be used commercially'; free academic license at backend.totalsegmentator.com/license-academic, commercial license via jakob.wasserthal@usb.ch. Verified web search 2026-06 + docs/license_risk_table.md (tissue stats Proprietary).
notes: Nuanced: base 'total' (117 classes) model+code ARE Apache-2.0 and commercially usable. But the high-resolution and several specialized subtask models are explicitly non-commercial (license/commercial-license required), which is the audited landmine ('high-res NC'). Because the package treats this as one restricted entry covering the NC high-res/tissue/face subtasks, keep external_baseline_only and do NOT bundle those weights. A future split could route ONLY the Apache-2.0 'total' task into a medical extra after legal review; the NC subtasks must remain out. Acquiring the free academic license is a separate registration (non-commercial only), not an HF gate.

provenance: github.com/ultralytics/ultralytics (AGPL-3.0); ultralytics.com/license; HF Ultralytics/YOLO* model cards all AGPL-3.0. Verified web search 2026-06.
notes: All Ultralytics YOLO trained models are AGPL-3.0 by default. Commercial closed-source use requires a paid Ultralytics Enterprise License (hence 'conditional', NOT free). Under AGPL alone, integrating into a commercial package forces full source disclosure. Cannot mirror/bundle into permissive core; external baseline only.

provenance: github.com/AILab-CVC/YOLO-World (LICENSE = GPL-3.0, Tencent AI Lab); also integrated in Ultralytics under AGPL-3.0. README notes business inquiries to Tencent. Verified web search 2026-06.
notes: Open-vocabulary detector. Original Tencent repo is GPL-3.0 (README informally says 'supported for commercial usage' but GPL copyleft obligations still apply); Ultralytics path is AGPL-3.0. Either way strong copyleft -> commercial use only via separate licensing/source disclosure ('conditional'). Pretrained weights also trained on large open-vocab corpora (O365/GoldG/CC) raising dataset questions. Exclude from permissive core; external baseline only.

provenance: github.com/Deci-AI/super-gradients (code Apache-2.0) + YOLONAS.md; pretrained weights under Deci's separate non-commercial weights license. HF discussions confirm weights not free for commercial use. Deci acquired by NVIDIA, unmaintained. Verified web search 2026-06 + tools/audit_libreyolo_hf_models.py.
notes: Classic CODE-vs-WEIGHTS split: super-gradients library is Apache-2.0, but the YOLO-NAS pretrained checkpoints (AutoNAC-generated) carry Deci's restrictive non-commercial license. So even though code is permissive, the published weights forbid commercial use -> commercial_use_allowed=no and cannot be mirrored/bundled. 'libreyolo-yolonas' is the VisionServeX wrapper name. External baseline only (architecture may be retrained from scratch on commercial data, but the published weights stay out).
